definition.meaning: 1. Fingernail or toenail (original sense). 2. Small metal spike or peg for fastening.
definition.origin: From Proto-West Germanic *nagl, from Proto-Germanic *naglaz, from PIE *h₃nogʰ- "nail"

definition.meaning: To fix or fasten something onto something else with nails

definition.cognates: Old Saxon negil, Old Norse nagl, Old High German nagal, German Nagel, Gothic ganagljan
Latin unguis, Greek onyx, Sanskrit nakha

definition.origin: From Middle Irish tairnge, from Proto-Celtic *tarankyos "nail", from PIE *terh₁- "to drill, rub"
From Old Irish inga, from Proto-Celtic *angʷīnā, from PIE *h₃nogʰ- (same root as English nail)

definition.meaning: Ancient Germanic personal name based on element nah "sufficient, adequate"
definition.usage: Surname; also metonymic occupational name for a maker of nails, or nickname for a tall slender person

definition.origin: From PIE *h₃nogʰ- (same root as English nail, Old English naegl)
